name: Pre-Transcode
description: Proactively pre-transcodes library media in the background into an admin-defined compatibility baseline, eliminating repeated live CPU transcoding for the same files on every playback. Every encoding parameter is configurable and populated from what the server's ffmpeg supports.
overview: Pre-transcode media to an admin-defined compatibility baseline.

versions.changelog: Two requested features. Profiles gain a "Keep original if the transcode is not smaller" option: after an encode is verified, if the new file is not smaller than the source it is discarded and your original is kept untouched — useful when re-encoding to a more efficient codec that can still produce a bigger file on some content, with no manual per-file size comparison across the library. The source is then remembered as handled, so the automatic sweep does not re-encode it on every run. Also adds a new VideoDurationMinutes trigger-rule condition, so a file-size threshold can be scaled by video length instead of a single blanket size — e.g. "duration under 30 min AND size over 350 MB".

Adds on-demand single-item pre-transcoding. From the Queue & status page there is now a "Transcode a single item" panel: search for a movie or episode by name, pick an encoding profile, and queue just that one — independent of the trigger rules and the automatic sweep (it is still skipped at run time if the item is already compliant). Also fixes the queue list appearing frozen during a slow (e.g. 4K) encode: the progress bar, elapsed time and estimated time remaining now keep updating while a job runs, instead of only redrawing when the whole-number percent changed.

A broad correctness, safety and performance release from a full code audit. Audio: the channel cap now actually downmixes (e.g. 5.1 to stereo) — it was silently ignored, which under replace-in-place also re-transcoded the file endlessly. Subtitles: mp4/mov outputs keep their text subtitles (as mov_text) instead of dropping them, and webm drops only the image subtitles it cannot store rather than failing the encode. Resolution: an odd cap (e.g. 1281) no longer aborts the encode. Encoders: VAAPI/VideoToolbox no longer receive a -preset they reject. Data safety: paths are compared case-sensitively off Windows so replace-in-place cannot overwrite an unrelated same-name file, and sibling/separate-directory outputs are written crash-safely. Rules and library: a half-configured rule no longer matches the whole library, files with no audio or an empty-container profile are no longer re-transcoded every sweep, embedded cover art is no longer mistaken for the video stream, and the sweep survives a bad item. Queue: pausing can no longer leave an encode permanently frozen, a just-started job's cancel is no longer lost, and ffmpeg is killed on cancel or shutdown; the encoder-preset cache is now thread-safe and a large library import no longer does O(n^2) work. Also: tighter Dolby Vision detection and UTF-8 decoding of ffmpeg output.

Fixes TV episodes appearing twice in Jellyfin when using the "add as alternate version" output mode. The transcode is linked to its source as an alternate version via a Jellyfin database link, but the plugin fired one merge per completed episode and forced a full library scan each time; those scans re-indexed the freshly-written file concurrently with the merge, and with no locking it was last-writer-wins, so some episodes collapsed to one entry and others were left as a duplicate. Merges are now serialized and no longer force a scan, and the link is applied to the canonical item. Note: Jellyfin's filename version grouping is movies-only, so TV relies on this link; if a duplicate remains, merge the two entries once from the dashboard or use replace-in-place.

Reliability, safety and performance release. Stops the same episode being queued and transcoded more than once (the duplicate "... (1)" outputs). Pausing the queue now also pauses the running encode and resumes it where it left off. Replace-in-place is crash-safe across Docker mounts and never overwrites an unrelated file. "copy" video profiles no longer re-transcode forever; compliance and rules now consider every audio track, not just the first. Removing or cancelling a running job now actually stops its ffmpeg. Security hardening for ffprobe/ffmpeg arguments and input protocols. Performance: ffprobe results are cached so unchanged files are not re-probed every sweep, and the sweep no longer scales with the square of the library size.

Fixes mp4 sources failing within seconds of starting whenever the output container is Matroska. Subtitles in an mp4 are mov_text, which Matroska cannot store, so copying them made the muxer reject the output header and abort the whole transcode (ffmpeg exit code 218, "Function not implemented"). Subtitle tracks are now handled individually: those the output container can store are copied verbatim, and the rest are converted to a text format it can (srt, or WebVTT for webm). mp4 sources therefore transcode successfully and keep their subtitles instead of losing them. Failed jobs on the queue page now expose the ffmpeg output that caused the failure.

Fixes the automatic library sweep re-transcoding files it had already produced output for. In the separate-directory and add-as-alternate-version modes the original source is left unchanged, so every daily sweep re-queued and re-encoded the same files, piling up duplicate outputs. A source is now skipped when the profile's expected output already exists on disk (robust — this survives clearing the job queue) or the queue records a completed transcode for it, and a source that keeps failing is no longer auto-retried after 3 attempts (it can still be requeued manually).

Settings page redesign for clarity: the profile editor is grouped into labelled Video / Resolution / Audio / HDR / Output sections, fields that don't apply are hidden (e.g. CRF only in constant-quality mode, the alternate-version label only for that output mode), every field carries an inline explanation, and advanced ffmpeg arguments are tucked into a collapsed section. The encoder preset is now a real dropdown when ffmpeg enumerates presets (e.g. libx264/x265), falling back to a free-text field with suggestions otherwise. The Queue & status page gains a "Cancel all" button that stops every pending and processing job at once.

Preserves every audio track (all languages) on transcode: a track already in the target codec is copied losslessly, the rest are re-encoded, and each is downmixed only if it individually exceeds the channel cap. For Matroska (mkv) outputs, all subtitle tracks and embedded fonts (for ASS/SSA) are carried across as well. Fixes resolution presets and trigger rules multiplying on every server start/update (constructor-seeded defaults were appended to, rather than replacing, the saved configuration on each load); pre-existing duplicates are now cleaned up automatically on load. Newly-added items still being copied or downloaded are now deferred and re-checked until the file settles, so "queue new items automatically" fires shortly after a file lands instead of waiting for the daily sweep. The "Add as alternate version" output mode now registers the transcoded copy as a proper Jellyfin alternate version (a Merge-Versions database link, source kept as primary) instead of only placing a companion file, so the original and the transcode appear as one movie with a version selector without renaming the original.

Adds a "Scan library now" button (plugin page and queue page) so a sweep can be triggered on demand instead of waiting for the schedule; puts the queue/status page in the dashboard sidebar as its own entry; and shows elapsed time plus an estimated time remaining for running jobs (and total duration for finished ones). Sweeps are now guarded so the scheduled task, the post-scan hook and the button can never overlap.

The "add as alternate version" output mode now uses a configurable version label. Output is named "<original> - <label>.<ext>", which is Jellyfin's native naming convention for alternate versions, so your label (e.g. "H.264 1080p") is what appears in Jellyfin's version selector instead of a hardcoded "pretranscode".

Initial release: dynamic ffmpeg-driven configuration, rules engine, per-library overrides, persistent job queue + verifying worker, library-scan hook, item-added monitor and a daily sweep scheduled task.
